qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded Decompression (Compression Bomb) in OpenPGP Compressed Data Packet Parsing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: Denial of Service via Resource Exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is stack overflow from recursive handling of deeply nested message layers and many signatures in the composed message parser/reader path; this finding concerns unbounded decompression in src/packet/compressed_data.rs.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Excessive S2K Iteration Count Causing CPU Exhaustion in Password-Protected Key/Message Processing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: Denial of Service via Cryptographic Resource Consumption
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is stack overflow from recursive handling of deeply nested message layers and many signatures; this finding concerns high S2K iteration counts in src/types/s2k.rs during password derivation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-8h58-w33p-wq3g is a stack-overflow crash in rPGP message parsing for deeply nested message layers, especially many nested signatures; the upstream fix rewrites the composed message parser and signed-message readers to avoid recursive signature/message handling. The final qualifying findings instead cover unbounded compressed-data decompression and excessive S2K password-derivation iterations.
evidence: Advisory summary/details: rPGP versions before 0.19.0 can crash with stack overflow when parsing messages containing deeply nested message layers such as many signatures.

Patch e82f2c7494ba277d62fd372d69b2c008473bbef8 is titled 'fix: recursion issues in deeply nested messages' and changes src/composed/message/parser.rs plus signed-message reader implementations, including creation of src/composed/message/reader/signed_many.rs.
Session output.scanned and final validation list exactly two final vulnerability folders: dos_compression_bomb and dos_s2k_iteration_count.
dos_compression_bomb reports src/packet/compressed_data.rs:156-159 and decompression output without size limits, not recursive message/signature parsing.
dos_s2k_iteration_count reports src/types/s2k.rs iteration-count decoding and password KDF CPU exhaustion, not recursive message/signature parsing.
